Package neqsim.physicalproperties.methods.liquidphysicalproperties.density

Class Costald

java.lang.Object
neqsim.physicalproperties.methods.PhysicalPropertyMethod
neqsim.physicalproperties.methods.liquidphysicalproperties.LiquidPhysicalPropertyMethod
neqsim.physicalproperties.methods.liquidphysicalproperties.density.Costald
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Cloneable, DensityInterface, PhysicalPropertyMethodInterface, ThermodynamicConstantsInterface
public class Costald extends LiquidPhysicalPropertyMethod implements DensityInterface

COSTALD (Corresponding States Liquid Density) calculation for liquids.

Implements the Hankinson-Thomson (1979) method for saturated liquid molar volume and the Aalto et al. (1996) Tait-type correction for compressed (sub-cooled) liquids. The pseudocritical vapor pressure uses the Lee-Kesler correlation.

References:

  • Hankinson, R.W. and Thomson, G.H., AIChE J. 25, 653-663 (1979)
  • Thomson, G.H., Brobst, K.R. and Hankinson, R.W., AIChE J. 28, 671-676 (1982)
  • Aalto, M. et al., Fluid Phase Equil. 114, 1-19 (1996)
  • Poling, B.E. et al., The Properties of Gases and Liquids, 5th ed., McGraw-Hill (2001)
Version:
$Id: $Id
Author:
Even Solbraa

    • usePolarCorrection

      private boolean usePolarCorrection
      Whether to apply the NBS (NASTALD) polar correction of Thomson, Brobst & Hankinson (1982). When enabled, uses substance-specific polar parameters to improve accuracy for polar compounds (water, alcohols, glycols, ammonia).

      a PhysicalPropertyMethodInterface object

    • calcVR0

      private double calcVR0(double reducedTemperature)
      Calculates COSTALD dimensionless saturated volume function V_R^(0).

      V_R^(0) = 1 - 1.52816 tau^(1/3) + 1.43907 tau^(2/3) - 0.81446 tau + 0.190454 tau^(4/3) where tau = 1 - Tr.

      Parameters:
      reducedTemperature - reduced temperature T/Tc
      Returns:
      V_R^(0) dimensionless

    • calcVRdelta

      private double calcVRdelta(double reducedTemperature)
      Calculates COSTALD dimensionless departure volume function V_R^(delta).

      V_R^(delta) = (-0.296123 + 0.386914 Tr - 0.0427258 Tr^2 - 0.0480645 Tr^3) / (Tr - 1.00001)

      Parameters:
      reducedTemperature - reduced temperature T/Tc
      Returns:
      V_R^(delta) dimensionless

    • calcVRpolar

      private double calcVRpolar(double reducedTemperature)
      Calculates the NBS (NASTALD) polar correction function V_R^(p) from Thomson, Brobst & Hankinson (1982).

      V_R^(p) = (-0.093 - 0.03422 * Tr + 0.8326 * Tr^2 - 0.4572 * Tr^3) / (Tr - 1.00001)

      This function is used in the NASTALD modification where the saturated molar volume becomes: V_s = V* * [V_R^(0) * (1 - omega * V_R^(delta)) + omega_p * V_R^(p)] where omega_p is a substance-specific polar parameter.

      Parameters:
      reducedTemperature - reduced temperature T/Tc
      Returns:
      V_R^(p) dimensionless polar volume correction

    • getPolarParameter

      private double getPolarParameter(int compIdx)
      Gets the NASTALD polar parameter omega_p for a component.

      These polar parameters are from Thomson, Brobst & Hankinson (1982), Table 1. They correct COSTALD for strongly polar and hydrogen-bonding substances where the acentric factor alone cannot capture density behavior.

      Returns 0.0 for non-polar compounds (which disables the polar correction term for that component).

      Parameters:
      compIdx - component index in the phase
      Returns:
      omega_p dimensionless polar parameter (0.0 for non-polar compounds)

    • setUsePolarCorrection

      public void setUsePolarCorrection(boolean usePolar)
      Enables or disables the NBS (NASTALD) polar correction.

      When enabled, the Thomson-Brobst-Hankinson (1982) polar correction term is added for polar compounds (water, alcohols, glycols, ammonia). This can improve accuracy from ~3% to ~1% for strongly polar substances. The correction has no effect on non-polar hydrocarbons (their polar parameter is zero).

      Parameters:
      usePolar - true to enable polar correction, false to disable

    • isUsePolarCorrection

      public boolean isUsePolarCorrection()
      Returns whether the NASTALD polar correction is enabled.
      Returns:
      true if polar correction is active

    • calcDensity

      public double calcDensity()

      Returns the density of the phase. Unit: kg/m^3

      Calculates liquid density using the COSTALD method. Returns density in kg/m3.

      Specified by:
      calcDensity in interface DensityInterface
      Returns:
      The density of the phase in kg/m^3.

    • applyCompressedLiquidCorrection

      private double applyCompressedLiquidCorrection(double vsSat, double reducedTemperature, double omega, double vstarMix, double tcMix, double pressure)
      Applies the Aalto et al. (1996) compressed liquid correction.
      Parameters:
      vsSat - saturated liquid molar volume in cm3/mol
      reducedTemperature - reduced temperature T/Tc_m
      omega - acentric factor of mixture
      vstarMix - characteristic volume of mixture in cm3/mol
      tcMix - pseudocritical temperature of mixture in K
      pressure - system pressure in bar
      Returns:
      corrected liquid molar volume in cm3/mol

    • getCharacteristicVolume

      private double getCharacteristicVolume(int compIdx)
      Gets the COSTALD characteristic volume V* for a component.

      Priority order:

      1. Explicitly set V* via setCostaldCharacteristicVolume()
      2. Back-calculated from normal liquid density at 60 deg F (288.71 K) using the Hankinson-Thomson saturated volume equation solved inversely. This is used for TBP/plus fractions and polar/associating compounds (water, glycols, alcohols) where the critical volume is not a good estimate of V*. This is the standard approach in commercial simulators (UniSim/HYSYS, PRO/II).
      3. Fallback: critical volume Vc (for components without known liquid density, or when the back-calculation gives a supercritical reduced temperature at standard conditions)
      Parameters:
      compIdx - component index in the phase
      Returns:
      characteristic volume V* in cm3/mol

    • estimateVstarFromDensity

      private double estimateVstarFromDensity(int compIdx)
      Estimates the COSTALD characteristic volume V* from the known normal liquid density at standard conditions (60 deg F = 288.71 K = 15.56 deg C). This is the Hankinson-Thomson (1979) approach for compounds where V* has not been explicitly set.

      This method is essential for three categories of components:

      • TBP/plus fraction pseudo-components (always have normalLiquidDensity from user input)
      • Polar/associating compounds (water, methanol, ethanol, MEG, TEG, DEG, etc.) where the critical volume Vc significantly overestimates V* due to strong hydrogen bonding and intermolecular association. Using the back-calculated V* from density inherently captures these polar effects.
      • Any database component with a known standard liquid density (normalLiquidDensity > 0)

      The method solves: rho_std = M / (V* * VR0 * (1 - omega * VRdelta)) for V*, where VR0 and VRdelta are evaluated at Tr = 288.71/Tc.

      Parameters:
      compIdx - component index in the phase
      Returns:
      estimated V* in cm3/mol, or 0 if estimation fails (e.g., near-critical or supercritical at standard conditions with Tr > 0.9, missing data)
